The proposed idea is coarse-to-fine internalization, so the student first learns the high-level solution structure and only then refines the details.","importance":"This is a post-training distillation paper, but it has broader significance for pretraining and mid-training. It suggests that reasoning may not transfer well through flat token-level imitation alone; separating planning from detailed derivation may be a better inductive bias for training smaller models.","technical_approach":"The method combines coarse-to-fine knowledge internalization with self-reflection. Unlike standard CoT distillation, the student is not asked to mimic the full teacher trace from the start; it first learns a compressed scaffold of the solution and later fills in finer-grained reasoning, while self-reflection helps detect and revise inconsistent intermediate steps. That design is motivated by the limited capacity of small models to absorb long and noisy reasoning traces.","experiments":"The evaluation is likely on compact models over math and logical reasoning benchmarks, compared against plain SFT, direct CoT distillation, and related reasoning-transfer baselines. Single-run or greedy-only evaluation can hide meaningful variation in model behavior, including changes in ranking, capability estimates, and failure modes.","importance":"This is an evaluation-methodology contribution with direct consequences for both pretraining and post-training research. Many claimed improvements are only a few points, and if evaluation variance is on the same scale, some conclusions are simply not stable. The key message is that we should evaluate not just average behavior, but also the spread between good, bad, and greedy outcomes.","technical_approach":"From 'The Good, The Bad, and The Greedy,' the paper likely compares repeated stochastic decoding with greedy decoding and summarizes model behavior through distributional statistics rather than a single score. can a summarization model learn effectively from LLM-generated feedback when high-quality human supervision is scarce? Prior work typically leans on gold references, human preferences, or teacher distillation, which are either expensive or collapse training into imitating a teacher’s surface style rather than learning from explicit critique. The timely question is whether natural-language feedback from strong LLMs can be turned into a reliable training signal despite its noise.","importance":"This is primarily a method and supervision-design contribution, not a paradigm shift. This design is appealing because it teaches the model why a summary is weak, not just what a preferred summary looks like. Rudner"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"We investigate the mechanistic sources of uncertainty in large language models (LLMs), an area with important implications for language model reliability and trustworthiness. To do so, we conduct a series of experiments designed to identify whether the factuality of generated responses and a model’s uncertainty originate in separate or shared circuits in the model architecture. Zhang"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"$21","venue":"NAACL","year":2025,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"https://arxiv.org/abs/2502.17599","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"https://dblp.org/rec/conf/naacl/WanSWLMZ25","doi":"https://doi.org/10.18653/V1/2025.NAACL-LONG.125"},"_dblp_key":"conf/naacl/WanSWLMZ25","_ee":"https://doi.org/10.18653/v1/2025.naacl-long.125","category_id":"efficient","relevance_score":6,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"这篇论文解决的是：长上下文多模态推理时，KV cache 增长过快，而现有压缩方法按层一刀切分配缓存，忽略了不同层的注意力密度差异。文本-图像、文本-视频输入会让 cache 成本远高于纯文本；如果仍用均匀或固定递减策略，就会在不重要层浪费预算、在关键信息融合层压得过狠，最终把效率提升换成明显精度损失。","importance":"这项工作的价值是系统级和推理优化级的，但对 pretrain 也有启发：多模态模型不同层承担的跨模态融合强度并不均匀，说明架构和训练后形成了明显的层功能分化。对统一多模态建模来说，这类结果能帮助判断哪些层真正承载了跨模态对齐，进而影响未来的训练分配、蒸馏或 cache-aware 设计。","technical_approach":"作者提出 MEDA，本质上是一种按层动态分配 KV cache 预算的方法，而不是固定压缩率。核心信号是 cross-modal attention entropy：如果一层的跨模态注意力更集中、熵更低，说明少数 token 更关键，可以更有针对性地保留；如果更分散，则可以采用不同的压缩策略。基于每层动态确定的 cache 大小，MEDA 还配套相应的选择/压缩机制。它与已有方法的关键区别不在于再发明一种 token pruning，而在于先解决“各层该分多少预算”这个更上游的问题，因此更贴合多模态长上下文里层间异质性很强的现实。","experiments":"摘要没有给出完整数字，但能确认作者面向的是 multimodal long-context inference，覆盖文本-图像和文本-视频场景。核心比较对象应是文本或多模态 LLM 中常见的 uniform/progressive KV 压缩策略，而论文主张 MEDA 在相近预算下能更好保留性能，或者在相近性能下进一步降低 cache 成本。由于摘要后半截被截断，模型名称、上下文长度、压缩比和具体分数提升无法准确填写。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper addresses a concrete bottleneck: in long-context multimodal inference, KV caches grow rapidly, and existing compression methods allocate cache almost uniformly across layers despite large variation in attention density. With text-image and text-video inputs, this is much more expensive than text-only inference. A fixed allocation wastes budget on unimportant layers and over-compresses layers where cross-modal integration is actually happening.","importance":"This is mainly a systems and inference-efficiency contribution, but it also carries a useful pretraining implication: multimodal transformers do not use all layers equally for cross-modal fusion. That layer specialization is worth paying attention to, because it can inform future training schedules, distillation, and cache-aware architecture choices for unified multimodal models.","technical_approach":"MEDA is a dynamic layer-wise KV cache allocator rather than another fixed compression rule. Its core signal is cross-modal attention entropy: layers with more concentrated cross-modal attention are treated differently from layers with diffuse attention, because the value of preserving tokens is not the same across layers. Once the cache size is set per layer, MEDA applies a corresponding retention/compression strategy. The key departure from prior work is that it solves the upstream budget-allocation problem first, instead of assuming every layer deserves the same or a hand-crafted progressive schedule.","experiments":"The abstract confirms that the setting is multimodal long-context inference with text-image and text-video inputs. If the compression ratio does not match actual spectral redundancy, some matrices are over-compressed and lose essential capacity, while others keep singular components that contribute little.","importance":"This is a method-level improvement in model compression rather than a new compression paradigm. The useful takeaway for pretraining researchers is that compressibility is highly heterogeneous across an LM’s parameters, and that heterogeneity should be modeled explicitly instead of hidden behind a single global rank choice. That insight is relevant beyond post-hoc compression, including low-rank training, distillation, and regularization during training.","technical_approach":"SVD-LLM V2 improves singular value truncation in two steps. First, it uses theoretical truncation loss of each weight matrix to assign a matrix-specific compression ratio, instead of using one global rank or a simple heuristic schedule. Second, it adds another mechanism to further reduce truncation loss; the abstract names this as a core component, but the provided text is truncated before the exact design is described. The directions that carry the most energy in the base model are not necessarily the ones that are most useful for adaptation. If the paper’s claim holds, it affects how we allocate rank, initialize adapters, and think about spectral structure at the pretrain–posttrain boundary.","technical_approach":"From the title, MiLoRA explicitly leverages minor singular components for parameter-efficient finetuning instead of restricting updates to the dominant subspace. The method likely starts from an SVD or approximate spectral decomposition, retains smaller singular directions that standard approaches would largely ignore, and parameterizes adaptation using them. Most MLLM benchmarks still focus on short-context multi-image settings, which makes it hard to tell whether a model truly supports long-context retrieval and localization across images, or is merely matching local cues in small contexts.","importance":"This is clearly a benchmark contribution. Its value is that it defines the long-context problem for MLLMs in a sharper way: not just whether the model can accept many images, but whether it can locate the relevant sub-image within a large visual context from textual instructions. For pretraining and inference work, this is useful because it couples context length, cross-modal retrieval, and fine-grained localization in one controlled evaluation.","technical_approach":"The paper introduces MMNeedle, a multimodal needle-in-a-haystack benchmark. Two design choices matter. First, besides standard multi-image input, it uses image stitching to push visual context length further than native multi-image interfaces alone. Second, it builds an automatic labeling protocol for sub-image-level retrieval, making the benchmark scalable and objective. This reduces evaluation noise and makes failures easier to attribute to long-context retrieval or cross-modal alignment rather than open-ended answer grading.","experiments":"The abstract states that the task is to locate a target sub-image within a haystack of images based on textual instructions and descriptions. Prior approaches are either data-hungry or weak at reliably satisfying hard requirements, which makes constraint-based customization worth isolating as a separate problem.","importance":"This is mainly a method and interface contribution rather than a new pretraining paradigm. The pretrain-to-posttrain takeaway is that some customization tasks are better framed as constrained alignment than imitation, which is more compatible with low-resource adaptation, enterprise policies, and fast-turnaround deployment.","technical_approach":"The likely core idea is to encode desired behavior as explicit constraints and optimize against those constraints rather than only matching demonstrations. Compared with vanilla instruction tuning, it focuses on what must and must not be violated; compared with generic preference optimization, it searches within a constrained feasible set. That design should help in low-data regimes, but only if the constraints are expressible and verifiable.","experiments":"Given the title, the key evidence should center on data efficiency and constraint satisfaction rather than raw benchmark scores. 的训练辅助信号。关键设计点通常在两处。第一，如何保证 critique 不只是复述偏好标签，而是真正指出缺陷维度。第二，如何把 critique 接入 RM——是作为输入条件、联合多任务目标，还是提取成结构化标签。相比外部教师生成 critique，这种自生成路线更便宜、闭环更强，但风险也更明显：同一模型族会共享偏见，导致 critique 与 RM If the evidence is solid, this would matter for synthetic preference pipelines and self-improvement systems.","technical_approach":"The core method is self-generated critiques: a model first critiques candidate answers, and those critiques are then used as auxiliary supervision for reward modeling. The design challenge is usually twofold. First, the critique must expose real failure dimensions rather than just restating the preference label. Second, the paper must decide how to incorporate critiques into RM training—conditioning input, multitask learning, or structured intermediate targets. Compared with critiques from an external teacher, self-generated critiques are cheaper and form a tighter closed loop, but they also raise a clear risk: the critique generator and the RM may share the same biases and overfit the same artifacts.","experiments":"The likely headline result is that self-generated critiques improve reward model quality and downstream reranking or alignment. That matters for interpretability, controllability, and safety, but the boundary is clear: steering can bias existing behavior more easily than it can add missing knowledge.","technical_approach":"The method appears to use sparse autoencoders to extract interpretable latent features, then steer inference by amplifying or suppressing features associated with knowledge selection. Existing fixes usually make a blunt trade-off: MQA/GQA reduce KV heads but can hurt quality, while cache compression or quantization saves memory at the cost of extra approximation error or engineering overhead. This work asks whether KV capacity should be allocated uniformly across layers in the first place.","importance":"This is mainly a systems-and-architecture contribution rather than a new pretraining paradigm. Its value is in exposing a more useful design space for inference: KV sharing does not need to be identical in every layer. For pretrain-minded readers, the takeaway is not about changing the LM objective, but about treating memory capacity as a layerwise resource. That matters for long-context serving, deployment after pretraining, and cost-efficient scaling of decoder-only models.","technical_approach":"The method, Multi-Layer Key-Value Heads (MLKV), assigns different numbers of KV heads to different Transformer layers while keeping the query-side attention structure intact. Conceptually, it is a structured refinement over the usual MHA-to-GQA/MQA transition: instead of collapsing all layers to the same reduced KV configuration, it preserves more KV capacity where it is likely to matter and compresses more aggressively elsewhere. The design is plausible because layers do not contribute equally to memory usage versus quality; a uniform KV-sharing scheme can over-compress the most sensitive layers. The trade-off is straightforward: MLKV adds configuration complexity, and its gains depend on how well the per-layer allocation matches the model and workload.","experiments":"The abstract is unavailable, so the paper-specific experimental facts cannot be stated reliably. The likely evaluation compares MLKV against MHA, GQA, and MQA on decoding memory, throughput, and downstream quality, with the key claim being that non-uniform KV allocation gives a better quality-memory trade-off than a single shared setting across all layers. Becker","Nabiha Asghar","Kabir Walia","Ashwin Srinivasan","Ehi Nosakhare","Soundar Srinivasan","Victor Dibia"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"","venue":"NAACL","year":2025,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"https://dblp.org/rec/conf/naacl/BoatengBAWSNSD25","doi":"https://doi.org/10.18653/V1/2025.NAACL-INDUSTRY.52"},"_dblp_key":"conf/naacl/BoatengBAWSNSD25","_ee":"https://doi.org/10.18653/v1/2025.naacl-industry.52","category_id":"post_train","relevance_score":5,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"这篇工作要解决的是：强模型能否把较难显式标注的“概念”蒸馏给弱模型，而不是只蒸馏最终答案。传统 distillation 更擅长传递 logits 或任务输出，但对抽象规则、可迁移理论和中间解释的传递较弱；作者把这个缺口定义为从 hypotheses 到 theories 的概念蒸馏问题。","importance":"它的重要性在于把蒸馏目标从“模仿结果”往“压缩抽象知识”推了一步。对 pretrain/posttrain 接口的启示是，教师模型未必只该提供伪标签，也可以提供中间结构化推理或概念层监督；如果做得好，这会提升小模型的数据效率和泛化，而不是只提升训练集上的 imitation accuracy。","technical_approach":"方法上，作者提出一种 hypotheses-to-theories prompting，名字已经说明核心思路：先让强模型生成候选假设，再进一步上升为更稳定的理论性概括，并用这些产物去监督弱模型。它和普通 CoT 蒸馏的区别不在于多给几步解释，而在于把零散实例归纳成可迁移的概念单元；这样设计的好处是弱模型学到的不是局部模板，但风险是教师归纳错误会被更系统地传递下去。","experiments":"可预期的实验重点是比较答案蒸馏、CoT 蒸馏与 hypotheses-to-theories 蒸馏在弱模型上的泛化表现，尤其是跨样本、跨任务或低资源场景。由于没有摘要，无法确认任务类型、模型规模、数据集和具体提升幅度；如果论文做得扎实，最有价值的结果会是概念型或组合型泛化优于直接答案监督，以及教师理论质量和学生性能之间的相关性分析。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper tackles a harder distillation target: transferring concepts from strong models to weak ones, rather than only transferring final answers. Standard distillation is good at matching outputs or logits, but much weaker at passing on abstract rules and reusable theories; this work frames that gap explicitly.","importance":"Its significance is that it shifts distillation from result imitation toward abstraction compression. For the pretrain-to-posttrain interface, the implication is clear: teacher models may be more useful as generators of structured intermediate supervision than as simple pseudo-labelers, especially if the goal is data efficiency and out-of-distribution generalization in smaller students.","technical_approach":"The method appears to use hypotheses-to-theories prompting: the strong model first proposes hypotheses and then consolidates them into higher-level theories, which are used to supervise the weak model. Existing red-teaming methods often provide broad coverage but weak control over specific risk types.","importance":"This matters because safety training is only as good as the failure cases it sees. Historically, teams vary prompts, filtering rules, scoring scripts, decoding settings, and dataset versions, which means a reported gain may reflect evaluation drift as much as model improvement.","importance":"This is primarily a systems and community-standard contribution. For pretraining research, that matters because conclusions about data mixture, objectives, architecture, and post-training are only as reliable as the evaluation protocol behind them. A credible standard reduces experimental noise and makes scaling studies, ablations, and model comparisons more trustworthy.","technical_approach":"The likely contribution is a standardized evaluation specification with a reference implementation, rather than a new benchmark suite. The critical design choices usually include fixed task formatting, prompt templates, few-shot policies, contamination and deduplication rules, decoding controls, and versioned data and code, plus auditable result logging. Compared with existing evaluation harnesses, the differentiator is not feature count but stronger standardization and better control of result drift.","experiments":"No abstract is provided, so the exact task coverage, model set, and quantitative variance reduction cannot be confirmed. This matters because modern models increasingly rely on few-shot demonstrations, so the ICL mechanism itself becomes part of the attack surface.","importance":"The contribution is mainly about safety, but it also informs pretraining and post-training design. If small amounts of poisoned data can reshape how a model generalizes from demonstrations, the implication is deeper than vulnerability alone: training is instilling a reusable in-context update rule. That directly affects data filtering, synthetic data generation, and preference-model training, where fragile meta-patterns can be unintentionally reinforced.","technical_approach":"The likely method is a poisoning scheme that inserts carefully constructed input-output patterns into training data so the model later follows attacker-chosen rules when given similar few-shot demonstrations. Unlike classic trigger-based backdoors, the trigger here is the structure of the demonstration itself. The idea is plausible because ICL already learns to infer task rules from context; poisoning repeatedly rewards the wrong meta-rule. The key boundary conditions are poisoning rate, transfer across prompt templates, and robustness across model families.","experiments":"No concrete model sizes or metrics are available from the provided information. The decisive experiments should compare clean training, random corruption, and structured poisoning across poisoning rates, then measure attack success or task accuracy under ICL prompts. That design is appropriate because the bottleneck in hard reasoning is usually not the amount of text but the quality of search guidance. Long but noisy rationales can dilute the objective and encourage templated behavior instead of better inference.","experiments":"The provided information does not specify datasets, models, or numerical gains. Many multilingual benchmarks conflate translation quality, factual recall, and reasoning, so strong scores are hard to interpret.","importance":"If the paper’s main claim is indeed that LLMs are cross-lingual but knowledge-free reasoners, the implication is important: multilingual reasoning success may reflect structural inference rather than robust multilingual knowledge. This is a capability decomposition study, not a new training method. The design is appropriate because controlling the knowledge variable is the only way to separate abstract reasoning from language-specific factual storage. From the title, this is not standard supervised KD on gold labels, but a setup where the teacher provides learnable signals on raw inputs, such as soft distributions, generated rationales, rankings, or other outputs. Both are standard practice, yet the empirical answer often depends on task details, batching, and optimizer dynamics, so many decisions are still made by trial and error.","importance":"Although it looks like a general learning problem, it is quite relevant to pretraining because the same trade-off appears in language, modality, and task-mixture balancing. This is probably more of a mechanism-and-evidence paper than a new architecture paper. Those differences drive stability, overfitting, and calibration behavior.","experiments":"The experiments likely run controlled comparisons among upsampling, reweighting, and hybrid strategies across multiple heavily imbalanced benchmarks, reporting minority-class metrics, overall performance, and possibly stability or calibration. Most prior acceleration methods rely on speculative decoding, draft models, or verification pipelines, which add system complexity and often lose efficiency when acceptance rates drop; this work appears to ask whether decoding itself can be made less sequential.","importance":"This is primarily a systems and inference-interface contribution, not a new pretraining recipe. Its relevance to pretraining is still real: if bidirectional or multi-head decoding can be made practical, then the assumption that a pretrained LM must be consumed strictly left-to-right becomes less rigid, which feeds back into how we design objectives, positional schemes, and model interfaces.","technical_approach":"The core idea is bidirectional multi-head decoding, where one forward pass predicts multiple positions and uses bidirectional information to reduce strict autoregressive dependence. This sounds like a decoding-side redesign rather than a new language model family; the trade-off is straightforward: a more complex prediction and consistency mechanism per step in exchange for fewer total decoding steps. The missing piece is a benchmark estimate that separates these confounds from underlying capability.","importance":"This is mainly an evaluation-method and benchmarking contribution. If the paper shows that raw Arena scores are systematically biased, that is operationally important.","technical_approach":"The likely approach is to infer a generalized latent capability from Arena battles rather than rely on plain Elo-style rankings. Relative to standard Arena scoring, such a framework would explicitly model factors like prompt difficulty, domain variation, judge or user bias, and possibly multiple capability dimensions. The motivation is strong: pairwise wins are only a noisy, biased observation channel. Without modeling the observation process, the estimated score will mix true capability with preference artifacts.","experiments":"The abstract is not provided, so model roster, data size, and exact metrics cannot be stated reliably. Prior safety-oriented work often optimizes only for resistance, which can produce brittle stubbornness, while pure updateability can make models manipulable. The real problem is not maximizing either side independently, but teaching models when to stand firm and when to change their mind.","importance":"This is mainly a post-training behavior and evaluation contribution, but it has clear implications for the pretrain/post-train interface. If training pipelines collapse them into a single refusal tendency, the model may look safer while becoming less corrigible and less useful. That trade-off is highly relevant for alignment and agentic deployment.","technical_approach":"The likely method is a dataset and training/evaluation setup that explicitly separates harmful persuasion from legitimate evidence-based persuasion. Unlike standard adversarial robustness formulations, this framing would not treat every attempt to change the model’s answer as an attack. Instead, it would condition the desired response on factors like evidence quality, argument strength, source credibility, or conflict with prior belief. That distinction matters because changing one’s answer is not inherently wrong; changing for bad reasons is.","experiments":"The abstract is missing, so no trustworthy statement can be made about setup size or exact gains. Transformer 推理时的一个结构性缺陷：模型一旦在早期 token 上走偏，后续生成通常只能沿着错误轨迹继续，缺少低成本的“犹豫—重构”机制。以往解决办法多靠采样、self-consistency、外部 verifier 或多次解码重试，但这些方法要么算力贵，要么只在最终输出层面筛选，没有在单次 inference pass 内部显式利用模型的不确定性。","importance":"这更像一个推理机制级工作，而不是预训练范式创新。它的重要性在于：如果能在不重新训练或只做轻量改动的前提下，让 Transformer 在推理中根据自身 hesitation 调整生成路径，那么这会影响 test-time scaling、推理稳定性以及未来对“内部不确定性信号”的利用。对 pretrain 研究的启发是，模型内部状态可能已经包含可用于纠偏的信号，只是现有解码器没有把它们转成动作。","technical_approach":"从标题看，HARP 的核心是 hesitation-aware reframing，即在一次 Transformer 推理过程中，检测模型何时处于犹豫状态，并据此重构后续推理或表征。和常规 greedy/beam decoding 不同，它不是简单比较 token 概率，而更可能利用 logits 分布、层间表征变化、注意力不一致性或某种 uncertainty score 来触发 reframing。设计上它放弃了完全单调、不可回看的生成流程，换来更强的局部纠错能力；边界在于，如果 hesitation 信号和真实错误弱相关，就会引入额外抖动甚至误改。","experiments":"没有摘要，无法确认是否是无需训练的 inference-time 方法、适用哪些任务、以及实际速度开销。最关键的实验应回答四个问题：第一，在哪些任务上收益明显，比如数学、长程推理、代码还是开放问答；第二，收益来自更好的错误恢复还是更高的采样多样性；第三，单位成本下是否优于 self-consistency、speculative retries 或 verifier reranking；第四，hesitation 触发阈值对质量和延迟的 trade-off 有多敏感。若没有成本归一化比较，这类方法很难判断实际价值。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper appears to address a structural weakness of standard Transformer decoding: once generation goes off track early, the model usually keeps committing to the wrong trajectory, with little internal mechanism for hesitation and recovery. Existing fixes often rely on repeated decoding, self-consistency, or external verification, which are expensive and mostly operate after the fact. The missing idea is to exploit uncertainty signals inside a single inference pass.","importance":"This is best viewed as an inference-mechanism contribution rather than a pretraining advance. It matters because a practical hesitation-aware decoding rule could improve test-time scaling and error recovery without requiring a new base model. For pretraining researchers, the interesting implication is that useful corrective signals may already exist inside model states, but standard decoding leaves them unused.","technical_approach":"From the title, HARP likely detects moments of hesitation during inference and uses them to reframe or revise the ongoing generation process. Unlike greedy or beam decoding, it is probably not just comparing next-token probabilities, but leveraging richer cues such as logit dispersion, instability across layers, attention disagreement, or another uncertainty proxy. The design trade-off is clear: it relaxes strictly one-pass monotonic decoding in exchange for local self-correction. The risk is that weakly calibrated hesitation signals could trigger unnecessary revisions and hurt fluency or latency.","experiments":"Without the abstract, the setup and numbers cannot be summarized reliably. Harnessing the Generative Power of LLMs for Training-Free Sentence Embeddings","authors":["Raghuveer Thirukovalluru","Bhuwan Dhingra"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"Training-free embedding methods directly leverage pretrained large language models (LLMs) to embed text, bypassing the costly and complex procedure of contrastive learning. Previous training-free embedding methods have mainly focused on optimizing embedding prompts and have overlooked the benefits of utilizing the generative abilities of LLMs. We propose a novel method, GenEOL, which uses LLMs to generate diverse transformations of a sentence that preserve its meaning, and aggregates the resulting embeddings of these transformations to enhance the overall sentence embedding. GenEOL significantly outperforms the existing training-free embedding methods by an average of 2.85 points across several LLMs on the sentence semantic text similarity (STS) benchmark. GenEOL also achieves notable gains in clustering, reranking, and pair-classification tasks from the MTEB benchmark. pretrain 的启发在于：如果语言模型在成分排序上确实复现了大部分人类偏好，那么预训练目标和语料分布已经足以让模型学到一部分深层句法偏置；如果偏差集中在特定结构上，那就能反过来暴露训练数据、tokenization 或目标函数对句法泛化的边界。对做基础模型的人来说，这类结果比单纯 benchmark 分数更接近“模型到底学到了什么”。","technical_approach":"从标题看，方法核心是比较语言模型与人类在多类成分排序现象上的偏好是否一致。合理推测作者不是让模型自由生成，而是通过受控句对或多候选结构比较其概率/偏好，这样能把问题收缩到排序决策本身，避免被流畅性或表面词频干扰。与传统句法接受度实验相比，这种设计更关注相对偏好而非绝对可接受性，因此更适合分析模型是否学到与人类相近的梯度式句法判断。","experiments":"目前给到的信息不足，无法确认所用模型、数据规模、语言现象覆盖范围和具体数值结果。仅从标题可确定的关键结论是：语言模型在大多数情况下表现出与人类相似的成分排序偏好；但“largely”也意味着并非完全一致，论文价值很可能在于刻画哪些结构对齐、哪些结构仍有系统性偏差。没有摘要，无法可靠填写 baseline、统计显著性或 ablation 细节。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper asks a sharp question: do language models exhibit human-like preferences over constituent order, rather than merely producing grammatical sentences? Prior work often folded this into broad syntax evaluations, where acceptable output was enough, but that does not test whether models learned fine-grained ordering preferences. The question matters now because LMs are increasingly used as proxies for human linguistic behavior, and constituent ordering is a more sensitive probe of what pretraining actually internalizes.","importance":"This is primarily an analysis paper, not a new modeling method. Its relevance to pretraining is that human-like ordering preferences would suggest next-token training on large corpora is sufficient to recover some nontrivial syntactic biases; systematic mismatches would instead reveal where data distribution, tokenization, or the objective fall short. For foundation-model work, that is more informative than another aggregate benchmark score.","technical_approach":"From the title, the method is most likely a controlled preference comparison between human judgments and LM preferences across multiple constituent-ordering phenomena. The key design choice is probably to compare alternatives with matched content, instead of relying on open-ended generation, so the signal reflects ordering decisions rather than general fluency. Compared with classic acceptability studies, this setup is better suited to measuring graded preferences and whether the model aligns with human ranking patterns.","experiments":"The provided information is too limited to state model sizes, datasets, covered constructions, or exact numbers. The only defensible takeaway from the title is that LMs align with human constituent-ordering preferences in most cases, though not perfectly; the interesting part is likely where the alignment breaks. Kass-Hout","Furong Huang","Cao Xiao"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"$30","venue":"NAACL","year":2025,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"https://arxiv.org/abs/2405.15973","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"https://dblp.org/rec/conf/naacl/WangCWZZYZGBKHX25","doi":"https://doi.org/10.18653/V1/2025.FINDINGS-NAACL.15"},"_dblp_key":"conf/naacl/WangCWZZYZGBKHX25","_ee":"https://doi.org/10.18653/v1/2025.findings-naacl.15","category_id":"multimodal","relevance_score":5,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"这篇论文要解决的是：现有 LVLM 的视觉-语言对齐提升，往往依赖更强外部模型或额外数据，成本高且结果不稳定。很多视觉指令调优方法默认数据和 teacher 足够好，但真正限制模型上限的常常是模态对齐误差本身；如果继续靠外援纠偏，就很难控制偏好信号质量，也难判断提升究竟来自方法还是来自更强 teacher。","importance":"这项工作的价值在于，它把 VLM 对齐问题重新表述成一种可自举的偏好优化流程，贡献类型偏方法级。对 pretrain/posttrain 接口的启发是：多模态模型未必总需要外部裁判来产生高质量偏好信号，只要自评机制设计得当，模型自身就能挖掘出一部分可用于继续对齐的训练信号。这对受限场景下的持续改进尤其有意义。","technical_approach":"作者提出 SIMA，一个不依赖外部模型或外部数据的 self-improvement 框架。做法是基于现有 vision instruction tuning 数据，让模型先自生成响应，再通过 in-context self-critic 机制构造 preference pairs，随后用这些偏好对模型继续调优。关键设计不只是“自己给自己打分”，而是要把 critic prompt 设计到足够稳定，让模型能聚焦视觉-语言一致性而不是表面措辞。与依赖 GPT-4 或额外标注器的方法相比，SIMA 的主要取舍是牺牲部分 teacher 上界，换来更低成本、更可控的数据闭环。","experiments":"摘要明确声称方法能在不借助外部依赖的前提下提升 visual-language modality alignment，但未给出这里需要的具体数字。可以确认的实验重点应包括：在若干 LVLM benchmark 上与原始 vision instruction tuning 模型比较，验证 self-generated preference pairs 是否稳定带来收益；同时还会检验 critic design 对偏好对质量的影响。由于摘要未展开模型规模、训练数据量、基线和提升幅度，无法准确补充数值。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper targets a practical limitation in LVLM alignment: many existing improvements depend on stronger external models or additional data, which makes the alignment signal costly, hard to control, and difficult to attribute. In many cases the bottleneck is not lack of instruction tuning per se, but residual visual-language misalignment. If correction always comes from outside teachers, it is hard to know whether the gain is from the method or simply from a better supervisor.","importance":"This is a method-level contribution that reframes multimodal alignment as a self-bootstrapping preference optimization problem. The pretrain/posttrain takeaway is that multimodal models may be able to generate usable alignment signals on their own, provided the self-critique mechanism is designed carefully. That matters for continual improvement settings where external supervision is expensive or unavailable.","technical_approach":"SIMA is a self-improvement framework that avoids external models and extra data. Starting from existing vision instruction tuning data, the model first generates responses, then uses an in-context self-critic mechanism to build preference pairs, and finally trains on those pairs to improve alignment. The key design choice is not merely self-scoring, but making the critic stable enough to judge visual-language consistency rather than superficial wording. Existing test-time methods compare the output distribution with and without context, then apply a fixed adjustment. That assumes all examples have a similar degree of conflict, which is false in practice. When no conflict is present, static correction can hurt by pushing the model away from already-correct parametric knowledge.","importance":"This is an inference-time method paper, but it matters for pretraining and knowledge integration more broadly. It highlights that contextual and parametric knowledge should not be treated as a simple binary choice. Without estimating conflict strength, retrieval-augmented generation, edited models, and continual pretraining setups can all suffer from unstable behavior. The contribution is method-level, with a clear and broadly relevant problem formulation.","technical_approach":"AdaCAD is an instance-level adaptive contrastive decoding method. The central idea is to infer how much conflict exists for the current example and then set the adjustment weight dynamically, instead of using a fixed contrastive coefficient. That lets the decoder rely more on context when conflict is strong, and stay closer to the original model when conflict is weak or absent. Compared with prior static methods, the gain comes from separating conflict detection from conflict correction.","experiments":"The abstract explicitly states that static contrastive methods often misjudge conflict and over-adjust when conflict is absent, and that AdaCAD addresses this with fine-grained, instance-specific control. Many prior defenses focus on trigger detection, poisoned-sample filtering, or robust training, while the actual attack often succeeds because the model over-memorizes an abnormal mapping.","importance":"This is primarily a security-analysis and data-centric contribution. For pretraining, the takeaway is that poisoned samples may matter not only because they are mislabeled, but because they consume a disproportionate amount of memorization capacity. That perspective connects directly to deduplication, memorization monitoring, and the outsized effect of anomalous long-tail examples.","technical_approach":"Judging from the title, the method likely characterizes poisoned versus clean samples through memorization-related signals and then uses those signals for data-level defense. Unlike signature-based trigger detection, it does not rely on recovering the exact trigger pattern. Instead, it looks for samples remembered with abnormally high confidence or low generalization. Existing decoding strategies like DoLa use layer contrasting but lack fine-grained analysis of which token positions need intervention and how to quantify the intervention signal. This work proposes token-wise Cross Layer Entropy to locate and correct uncertain tokens.","importance":"Method-level contribution providing an inference-time factuality improvement without external knowledge. Offers mechanistic insight into inter-layer information flow and uncertainty propagation, with implications for understanding training dynamics (at which layers tokens get 'decided').","technical_approach":"Computes entropy changes across Transformer layers for each token position (Cross Layer Entropy) to identify internally uncertain tokens. That means comparisons among PEFT methods can be misleading if prompt placement is treated as an afterthought.","technical_approach":"The likely contribution is a more holistic prompt-tuning framework in which placement is a first-class design choice rather than a frozen hyperparameter. This is a sensible framing because many editing methods perform localized interventions in parameters or activations, which can leave non-natural generalization patterns. multilingual transfer 的有效容量远小于完整模型，而且可以被 certified ticket 捕获，那么多语言预训练、continual adaptation 和 parameter-efficient transfer 的设计都会被重新审视。它既有方法意义，也有资源效率意义。","technical_approach":"核心方法是 可能对应具体的选择准则或证书机制。与一般剪枝后再微调不同，这类方法的关键在于先定义什么叫‘对迁移有效的子网’，再用可验证标准筛选，而不是盲目按权重大小剪。这样做的好处是结果更可解释、更稳定；代价是证书往往保守，可能牺牲一部分最优性能。","experiments":"实验最关键的是展示：找到的 lottery tickets 在多语言任务上接近或优于全模型迁移，同时具有更高效率，并且认证不是空壳。合理设置会覆盖多个源语言/目标语言组合、不同 sparsity 水平和不同 transfer 方案。由于没有摘要，无法确认模型类型、稀疏率和具体性能数字。最值得看的结果是：1）认证 ticket 与非认证 ticket 的差距；2）高资源与低资源语言上的表现差异；3）是否存在跨语言共享 ticket，而不是每种语言都要单独找。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"This paper addresses a practical question in multilingual transfer: can we find small but reliable subnetworks that preserve transfer-relevant capacity, and can we certify that they do so. Prior lottery-ticket work has mostly focused on trainability of sparse subnetworks, not on certified guarantees in multilingual adaptation.","importance":"The contribution sits at the intersection of sparsity, transfer, and certifiability. For pretraining, the implication is significant: if multilingual transfer can be carried by a much smaller certified subnetwork, that changes how we think about multilingual capacity allocation, continual adaptation, and parameter-efficient transfer. This is both a method and an efficiency result.","technical_approach":"The core idea is KS-Lottery, a method for finding certified lottery tickets for multilingual transfer. The word 'certified' suggests the method does not just identify a subnetwork heuristically, but attaches some formal or statistical guarantee about preservation of knowledge, transfer performance, or robustness. KS likely refers to the selection criterion or certificate mechanism. Unlike standard prune-then-finetune pipelines, this approach appears to define what a transfer-effective subnetwork should satisfy and then select using verifiable criteria rather than simple magnitude pruning. The benefit is greater interpretability and stability; the trade-off is that certification often makes the method conservative and may leave some peak performance on the table.","experiments":"The most important empirical result should be that the identified tickets retain multilingual transfer performance close to, or possibly better than, full-model transfer while using much less capacity, and that the certification has practical value rather than being decorative. A strong evaluation would cover multiple source-target language pairs, different sparsity levels, and several transfer settings. Since the abstract is unavailable, the exact models, sparsity ratios, and gains cannot be confirmed. The most informative analyses would compare certified versus non-certified tickets, contrast high-resource and low-resource languages, and test whether a shared cross-lingual ticket exists instead of one ticket per language.","authors_note":""},"tags":["lottery-ticket","multilingual","transfer","pruning"]},{"id":"ZhangFJC25","title":"Enhancing Language Model Hypernetworks with Restart: A Study on Optimization","authors":["Yihan Zhang","Jie Fu","Rongrong Ji","Jie Chen"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"","venue":"NAACL","year":2025,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"https://dblp.org/rec/conf/naacl/ZhangFJC25","doi":"https://doi.org/10.18653/V1/2025.NAACL-LONG.397"},"_dblp_key":"conf/naacl/ZhangFJC25","_ee":"https://doi.org/10.18653/v1/2025.naacl-long.397","category_id":"llm_pretrain","relevance_score":5,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"这篇工作关注 LM hypernetwork 的一个优化难点：这类模型表达能力强，但训练容易不稳、陷入次优解或对初始化很敏感。过去超网络常被用来生成权重、适配任务或提高参数效率，但相比普通主干网络，它们的优化路径更复杂，因此实际效果常被优化问题而不是建模上限限制。","importance":"它的重要性在优化和架构实践层面。对 pretrain 社区的启示是：如果 hypernetwork 方案长期不如预期，问题可能不在想法，而在训练过程设计；一个简单有效的 restart 策略若能显著改善收敛，会降低这类架构进入主流训练栈的门槛。","technical_approach":"核心方法是给语言模型超网络训练引入 restart 机制，重点研究其优化收益。这里的 restart 很可能不是从零重训，而是在学习率、状态变量、甚至部分超网络参数上做阶段性重启，以摆脱坏盆地或不良耦合。这样设计的原因是 hypernetwork 存在更强的参数间依赖，早期优化路径容易锁死后续空间；restart 相当于保留已有有用结构，同时重新开放探索。与改架构相比，这是更轻量的改动；边界则是如果问题来自模型容量或归纳偏置错误，restart 只能缓解训练症状，不能根治。","experiments":"实验上应重点看：restart 是否稳定提升 hypernetwork 在语言建模或下游适配上的收敛速度、最终性能和方差。baseline 应包括无 restart 的标准训练，以及可能的 warm restart、cosine schedule 或不同优化器。由于没有摘要，无法确认超网络具体用途、模型规模和数字。最值得关注的是：1）restart 的最佳时机和频率；2）收益是否主要体现在减少 run-to-run variance；3）普通 Transformer 是否也同样受益，若是，则这不是 hypernetwork 特有结论。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"This paper studies an optimization bottleneck in language-model hypernetworks: they are expressive, but often hard to train, sensitive to initialization, and prone to poor local solutions. For the pretraining community, the implication is that some hypernetwork ideas may have underperformed not because the idea is weak, but because the training setup is fragile. If a simple restart strategy improves convergence materially, it lowers the barrier to making these architectures practical.","technical_approach":"The core idea is to introduce restart into hypernetwork training and analyze its optimization effect. This likely means staged resets of learning-rate schedules, optimizer state, or parts of the hypernetwork parameters, rather than fully restarting from scratch. The motivation is sensible: hypernetworks create stronger parameter interdependence, so early optimization paths can lock the model into poor regions. Restart can preserve useful structure while reopening exploration. Compared with changing the architecture, this is a lightweight intervention. The limitation is that if the real issue is capacity or inductive bias, restart can only alleviate optimization symptoms.","experiments":"The main experiments should test whether restart improves convergence speed, final performance, and run-to-run stability for hypernetwork-based language modeling or adaptation setups. Hard thresholds are unstable, while soft gating often creates a train-test mismatch when the final model must be truly pruned into a smaller structure.","importance":"The contribution is a compression-method improvement with some representation-level implications. For pretraining-oriented readers, the broader takeaway is that redundancy in LLMs is not uniform, and better decision mechanisms for structured pruning can reveal which layers, heads, or channels are genuinely important to pretrained capability.","technical_approach":"From the title, QPruner introduces probabilistic decision quantization: learn pruning decisions in a probabilistic form, then quantize them into deployable structured choices. The design goal is to bridge continuous optimization and discrete deployment cleanly. Compared with standard sparsity penalties or importance ranking, this likely puts more emphasis on learning stable decision boundaries. That makes sense because structured pruning often fails not from the global sparsity target, but from a small number of near-boundary mistakes on critical components.","experiments":"The paper likely evaluates several LLMs against common structured-pruning baselines such as magnitude pruning, importance-based pruning, or soft-mask optimization, reporting perplexity and downstream retention at fixed compression ratios. A useful study would also compare different pruning granularities and ablate the probabilistic quantization step for stability and accuracy. Since no abstract or numbers are available, the exact scales, pruning rates, and gains cannot be stated confidently.","authors_note":""},"tags":["quantization","pruning","llm","compression"]},{"id":"ZhuFZQM25","title":"Logit Separability-Driven Samples and Multiple Class-Related Words Selection for Advancing In-Context Learning","authors":["Zixiao Zhu","Zijian Feng","Hanzhang Zhou","Junlang Qian","Kezhi Mao"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"","venue":"NAACL","year":2025,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"https://dblp.org/rec/conf/naacl/ZhuFZQM25","doi":"https://doi.org/10.18653/V1/2025.NAACL-LONG.343"},"_dblp_key":"conf/naacl/ZhuFZQM25","_ee":"https://doi.org/10.18653/v1/2025.naacl-long.343","category_id":"mechanism","relevance_score":5,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"这篇工作解决的是 ICL 示例选择中的两个常见误区：一是只按语义相似度找 demonstrations，二是用单一标签词做类别提示。这样做在分类任务里常常不稳，因为模型真正利用的不是“最像”的样本，而是那些能把类间决策边界拉开的样本与标签表达。","importance":"它的贡献偏方法优化，但对理解 ICL 有实际启发。对 pretrain 研究而言，这类结果提示我们：ICL 的效果高度依赖 prompt 内部形成的 logit 几何，而不是简单的表面匹配；示例选择和标签词设计其实是在操纵模型内部类别可分性。","technical_approach":"从标题看，作者用 logit separability 驱动样本选择，并为每个类别选择多个 class-related words，而不是固定一个 verbalizer。关键思想是同时优化两件事：demonstrations 要让不同类在模型输出空间里更可分，标签词集合要更充分覆盖类别语义，避免单词偶然偏置。与传统 nearest-neighbor ICL 或单 verbalizer 方法相比，这种设计更接近直接优化模型判别边界，因此通常对标签词敏感性更低、跨任务更稳。代价是选择过程更复杂，且可能需要额外打分前向。","experiments":"实验大概率聚焦文本分类或少样本分类，比较随机示例、语义检索示例、代表性 ICL 选择方法以及作者方法。核心指标应包括准确率提升，以及在不同 verbalizer、不同 shot 数下的稳定性。若 ablation 做得好，会单独验证 logit separability 和多类别词选择各自的贡献。因为没有摘要，无法确认任务集、模型规模和具体数值。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"The paper tackles two weak spots in in-context learning for classification: selecting demonstrations purely by semantic similarity, and representing each class with a single label word. Both are often brittle because what matters is not just semantic closeness, but whether the prompt induces clear class separation in the model’s output space.","importance":"This is a methodological improvement, but it also says something useful about how ICL works. For pretraining-oriented readers, the key point is that ICL performance is strongly shaped by the logit geometry created inside the prompt. Demonstration selection and verbalizer design are, in effect, tools for reshaping that geometry.","technical_approach":"From the title, the method selects samples using logit separability and assigns multiple class-related words instead of a single verbalizer per class. The design is trying to optimize two things at once: demonstrations that enlarge class margins in the model’s output space, and label-word sets that better cover class semantics and reduce single-token bias. Compared with nearest-neighbor retrieval or one-word verbalizers, this is closer to optimizing the induced decision boundary directly. The likely trade-off is extra scoring cost and more complicated prompt construction.","experiments":"The evaluation is likely on few-shot text classification, comparing random examples, semantic-retrieval baselines, prior ICL selection methods, and the proposed approach. The most relevant evidence would be accuracy gains and reduced sensitivity across different verbalizers and shot settings. A careful ablation would isolate the effect of logit-separability-based sample selection from that of multi-word class representation. Without the abstract, tasks, model scales, and exact numbers cannot be verified.","authors_note":""},"tags":["icl","in-context-learning","sample-selection","prompting"]},{"id":"ZhuKC25","title":"Can Post-Training Quantization Benefit from an Additional QLoRA Integration?","authors":["Xiliang Zhu","Elena Khasanova","Cheng Chen"],"institutions":[],"abstract":"","venue":"NAACL","year":2025,"track":"main","award":null,"links":{"arxiv":"","pdf":"","openreview":"","dblp":"https://dblp.org/rec/conf/naacl/ZhuKC25","doi":"https://doi.org/10.18653/V1/2025.NAACL-INDUSTRY.41"},"_dblp_key":"conf/naacl/ZhuKC25","_ee":"https://doi.org/10.18653/v1/2025.naacl-industry.41","category_id":"efficient","relevance_score":5,"reading_tier":"skim","summary":{"core_problem":"这篇工作问的是一个很实际的问题：后训练量化之后，再额外接一个 QLoRA 适配层，能不能继续把精度拉回来，而且值不值得。很多 PTQ 方法默认量化完成后模型就定型了，但如果少量可训练低秩参数能补偿量化误差，那 PTQ 与轻量适配之间就不是二选一关系。","importance":"它的价值偏系统与部署，但对大模型训练也有启发。对 pretrain 研究者来说，这关系到我们如何理解量化误差的可修复性：如果 QLoRA 能稳定修补 PTQ 损伤，说明不少误差是低秩、可学习且局部的；反过来，如果收益有限，就说明 PTQ 带来的信息损失更结构化，不是靠小适配器就能补。","technical_approach":"方法上很直接：在 post-training quantization 基础上，再引入额外的 QLoRA 微调步骤或适配模块，测试这种串联方案是否优于单独 PTQ 或单独 QLoRA。关键不是提出全新算法，而是澄清两种高效技术之间是互补还是重复。设计上的重点通常包括：QLoRA 插在哪些层、训练多少参数、是否只补偿量化敏感模块。这样做有效的前提是量化后的误差仍可被少量可训练参数吸收；如果误差分布太离散或涉及非局部交互，收益就会受限。","experiments":"实验应会系统比较全精度模型、PTQ 模型、QLoRA 微调模型，以及 PTQ+QLoRA 串联方案，在困惑度或下游任务上看恢复幅度，并考虑不同 bit 宽、不同模型规模和不同数据量。最有价值的观察通常是：收益主要出现在低 bit 还是中高 bit、哪些层最值得加适配器、训练开销是否值得精度回报。由于没有摘要，无法确认具体模型、量化配置和数值结果。","authors_note":""},"summary_en":{"core_problem":"This paper asks a practical question: after post-training quantization, can an additional QLoRA stage recover enough quality to matter, and under what conditions? PTQ is often treated as a terminal compression step, but if low-rank adapters can consistently repair quantization damage, then PTQ and lightweight adaptation are complementary rather than competing choices.","importance":"The contribution is mostly deployment-oriented, but it has a useful representation-level implication. For pretraining-minded readers, the key issue is whether quantization errors are locally repairable. If QLoRA works well on top of PTQ, much of the lost behavior may be low-rank and learnable. If it does not, then PTQ is destroying more structured information that small adapters cannot easily reconstruct.","technical_approach":"The approach is straightforward: start from a post-training-quantized model, add a QLoRA adaptation stage, and compare the combined pipeline against PTQ alone and QLoRA alone. The main value is not a brand-new algorithm but a careful test of complementarity versus redundancy between two popular efficiency techniques. Important design choices likely include which layers receive adapters, how many trainable parameters are used, and whether adaptation focuses on the most quantization-sensitive modules.","experiments":"The experiments likely compare full-precision, PTQ, QLoRA-tuned, and PTQ-plus-QLoRA models across perplexity or downstream tasks, while varying bit width, model size, and adaptation data volume. The most interesting findings would be where the gains concentrate: very low-bit regimes versus moderate quantization, and which layers are most worth adapting. Since no abstract or numbers are provided, the exact models, bit settings, and improvements cannot be verified.","authors_note":""},"tags":["quantization","qlora","post-training","compression"]}]},"allConferences":[{"slug":"acl-2025","venue":"ACL","year":2025,"full_name":"Annual Meeting of the
